evidenceBoundary.summary: This trace records observable repository work to restore source-panel code-block height synchronisation. It does not include private chain-of-thought.

operatingModel.branchPolicy: fix/ branches require audit trace artefacts under docs/agent-audit/reasoning/YYYY/MM/DD.

problemSummary: The previous generated-docs fix overcorrected the examples source panel layout by removing the max-height behaviour for example code blocks.
This broke the agreed source-panel prototype behaviour where the code block aligns to the accompanying text panel height, with a maximum code-panel height of 1400px and internal scrolling.
workSummary: Created fix/github-source-panel-height-cap from current main after PR #277 was merged.
Restored the generated source-panel layout so examples use the same code block behaviour as other source families.
Removed the example-specific CSS and JavaScript override that set max-height to none and overflow-y to visible.
Set the source-panel asset version to source-panel-height-cap-v3 to invalidate cached generated assets.
Updated build.sh checks to assert the restored 1400px max-height and overflow-y:auto behaviour rather than the removed full-source override.
Preserved the generated examples page checks so the page still has to include representative expanded examples.

staticChecksAdded: build.sh checks max-height: 1400px !important in the generated source-panel CSS.
build.sh checks overflow-y: auto !important in the generated source-panel CSS.
build.sh checks SOURCE_PANEL_CODE_MAX_HEIGHT = 1400 in the generated source-panel JavaScript.
build.sh checks the cache-busted source-panel-height-cap-v3 CSS and JS asset URLs.
build.sh still checks representative examples in source/examples/index.html.
notRunLocally: No local build command was run in this connector-only environment. CI should run build.sh on the pull request.

risks.risk: Long code examples will again use internal scrolling rather than expanding the full page height.
risks.mitigation: This is intentional and restores the agreed source-panel design contract. The examples page still includes representative source files and exposes code within the scrollable code panel.
